title: HTTP Semantics, Messages, Versions, and the Wire Model · Runtime Contract: Version Conformance
summary: A raw implementation copies endpoint behavior into its parser, confuses resources with representations, infers message bodies from method names, and treats HTTP/1.1 syntax as universal HTTP semantics. Planned artifact: Add and verify the version conformance increment in version-neutral message and semantics model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret HTTP version as capability and conformance information while rejecting unsupported major versions and unsafe downgrade assumptions.
Determine content meaning and permission from request method, response status, request context, and connection mode rather than one field alone.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Version Conformance · Theory Version Conformance
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 9126 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to derive message content from method and status semantics before wire framing; preserve this operating constraint: a timeout must reserve cleanup time and retain the terminal phase; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Interpret HTTP version as capability and conformance information while rejecting unsupported major versions and unsafe downgrade assumptions.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Version Conformance contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Build Version Conformance evidence · Theory Version Conformance
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: httpproto-version-conformance-fy7jy6" that uses case 4030 to derive message content from method and status semantics before wire framing. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets or listeners, negotiate TLS, contact networks or proxies, capture packets, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Go 1.26 protocol transfer gates.
